decisions.note: Text says approval should follow the Quality Management Plan; this is a precondition for S8/S9, not a precondition to them. Type should be 'postcondition' or 'guard' describing what must be true before approval happens, but as written it describes the process itself.
Source text (2.3): 'whenever procedures are changed, SOPs should be updated and re-approved.' The prescriptive force here is stronger than a suggestion—this is a standing requirement, not optional.
Source text (2.3): 'SOPs need to remain current to be useful. Therefore... SOPs should be also systematically reviewed on a periodic basis.' This is mandatory maintenance, not discretionary.
Source text (2.5): 'Each organization should develop a numbering system...' The word 'should' here is prescriptive for organizational policy; it is not optional.
